Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (sexual exploitation)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (sexual exploitation)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to increase sentences for offences of sexual exploitation and to add as an aggravating circumstance the fact that the victim is a person with a disability for the purpose of sentencing.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Paragraph 153(1.1)(b) of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 153.1(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 286.1:

When a court imposes a sentence for an offence referred to in subsection 286.1(1) or (2), it shall consider as an aggravating circumstance the fact that the victim of the offence is a person with a mental or physical disability.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (2) to (4) apply if Bill C-75, introduced in the 1st session of the 42nd Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Criminal Code, the Youth Criminal Justice Act and other Acts and to make consequential amendments to other Acts (in this section referred to as the âother Actâ), receives royal assent.
If section 54 of the other Act comes into force before section 2 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 2 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 153.1(1)(a) and (b) of the CrimÂ­inal Code are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.

Subsection.Text: If section 2 of this Act comes into force before section 54 of the other Act, then that section 54 is repealed.
If section 2 of this Act comes into force on the same day as section 54 of the other Act, then that section 54 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.
